root_text.sn16.4:1.3: kathaṁrūpo bhikkhu arahati kulūpako hotuṁ, kathaṁrūpo bhikkhu na arahati kulūpako hotun”ti?
root_text.sn16.4:2.1: “Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁcitto kulāni upasaṅkamati:
root_text.sn16.4:2.7: Tassa ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno evaṁcittassa kulāni upasaṅkamato na denti, tena bhikkhu sandīyati; so tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:2.10: dandhaṁ denti, no sīghaṁ, tena bhikkhu sandīyati; so tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:2.11: Asakkaccaṁ denti, no sakkaccaṁ; tena bhikkhu sandīyati; so tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:2.12: Evarūpo kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na arahati kulūpako hotuṁ.
root_text.sn16.4:3.1: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁcitto kulāni upasaṅkamati:
root_text.sn16.4:3.8: Tassa ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno evaṁcittassa kulāni upasaṅkamato na denti; tena bhikkhu na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:3.9: Thokaṁ denti, no bahukaṁ; tena bhikkhu na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:3.10: Lūkhaṁ denti, no paṇītaṁ; tena bhikkhu na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:3.11: Dandhaṁ denti, no sīghaṁ; tena bhikkhu na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:3.12: Asakkaccaṁ denti, no sakkaccaṁ; tena bhikkhu na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:3.13: Evarūpo kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu arahati kulūpako hotuṁ.
root_text.sn16.4:4.8: Tassa ce, bhikkhave, kassapassa evaṁcittassa kulāni upasaṅkamato na denti; tena kassapo na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:4.9: Thokaṁ denti, no bahukaṁ; tena kassapo na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:4.10: Lūkhaṁ denti, no paṇītaṁ; tena kassapo na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:4.11: Dandhaṁ denti, no sīghaṁ; tena kassapo na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:4.12: Asakkaccaṁ denti, no sakkaccaṁ; tena kassapo na sandīyati; so na tatonidānaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedayati.
root_text.sn16.4:4.13: Kassapena vā hi vo, bhikkhave, ovadissāmi yo vā panassa kassapasadiso. Ovaditehi ca pana vo tathattāya paṭipajjitabban”ti.

translation_text.sn16.4:1.3: What kind of mendicant is worthy of visiting families? And what kind of mendicant is not worthy of visiting families?”
translation_text.sn16.4:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn16.4:2.2: ‘May they give to me, may they not fail to give.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.3: May they give a lot, not a little.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.4: May they give me fine things, not coarse.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.5: May they give quickly, not slowly.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.6: May they give carefully, not carelessly.’
translation_text.sn16.4:2.7: If a mendicant with such a thought approaches a family and they don’t give, the mendicant feels slighted. And they experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.8: If they give only a little …
translation_text.sn16.4:2.11: if they give carelessly, the mendicant feels slighted. And they experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:2.12: That kind of mendicant is not worthy of visiting families.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.1: Whoever visits families with the thought:
translation_text.sn16.4:3.2: ‘When among other families, how could I possibly think:
translation_text.sn16.4:3.3: “May they give to me, may they not fail to give.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.4: May they give a lot, not a little.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.5: May they give me fine things, not coarse.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.6: May they give quickly, not slowly.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.7: May they give carefully, not carelessly.”’
translation_text.sn16.4:3.8: If a mendicant with such a thought approaches a family and they don’t give, the mendicant doesn’t feel slighted. And they don’t experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.9: If they give only a little …
translation_text.sn16.4:3.12: if they give carelessly, the mendicant doesn’t feel slighted. And they don’t experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:3.13: That kind of mendicant is worthy of visiting families.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.1: Kassapa visits families with the thought:
translation_text.sn16.4:4.2: ‘When among other families, how could I possibly think:
translation_text.sn16.4:4.3: “May they give to me, may they not fail to give.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.4: May they give a lot, not a little.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.5: May they give me fine things, not coarse.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.6: May they give quickly, not slowly.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.7: May they give carefully, not carelessly.”’
translation_text.sn16.4:4.8: With such a thought, if he approaches a family and they don’t give, he doesn’t feel slighted. And he doesn’t experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.9: If they give only a little …
translation_text.sn16.4:4.12: if they give carelessly, he doesn’t feel slighted. And he doesn’t experience pain and sadness because of that.
translation_text.sn16.4:4.13: I will exhort you with the example of Kassapa or someone like him. You should practice accordingly.”
